_comment: GENERATED baseline manifest snapshot — regenerate with `mars manifests`, never hand-edit. To change the plan, edit inputs/ and regenerate; see docs/manifests/README.md.

windows.objective: Loss-tolerant precursor: prove EDL, power, pilot ISRU, ECLSS testbed

requirements_closed.statement: The base shall operate autonomously under a 4-22 minute one-way light delay.
A surface-to-Earth communications link shall operate from the first window.
The program shall demonstrate survivable EDL of a 100 t-class cargo ship on Mars.
The program shall confirm and exploit accessible water ice (prospect, excavate, process).
Pressurized habitat volume beyond the landed ships shall be ready before crew.
Cargo offload, power distribution, and site logistics shall be operational from the first window.
Surface mobility for cargo and instruments shall be operational from the first window.
Follow-on ships shall land within meters of the base (precision landing aids operating).
The base shall have continuous, dust-storm-tolerant baseload power.
Starship shall reach orbit and be recovered.
Both Starship stages shall be reused (routine refly).
Orbital propellant transfer (refill) shall be demonstrated.
Long-duration cryogenic propellant storage (active chill) shall be demonstrated.
At least 5 successful cargo landings shall be accumulated before crew commits.
The ECLSS demonstration article shall be landed in the first window (demo clock).
The life-support demonstration shall run as an integrated article: the ECLSS loop operating inside a landed, pressurized habitat, both on the surface for the full 1,000-sol clock.
An integrated pilot chain shall run at 40 kg/hr propellant-equivalent or more in the first window.
Installed generation shall reach 500 kWe or more in the first window.
An Earth-entry thermal protection system rated for ~11.6 km/s interplanetary-return entry (~8x the heat load of a ~5.7 km/s Mars arrival) shall be qualified.
Mars ascent and trans-Earth injection (~6.5 km/s from the surface, single stage, no Mars-orbit refueling) shall be qualified.

Transfer and loading of ISRU-produced methalox into the return vehicle, and its cryogenic hold through the surface wait, shall be demonstrated; the return flight shall carry propellant samples for Earth-side verification.

windows.objective: Fuel factory x2: double rate-matched ISRU + the survival bridge, pre-landed

requirements_closed.statement: A near-closed life-support loop shall be demonstrated on Mars for 1,000 sols before crew.
The program shall produce and store a full crewed-return propellant load (1,400 t methalox) before crew commits.
A storm shelter and radiation protection shall be in place before crew.
Chain nameplate rate shall reach one return load per synod (>= 112 kg/hr).
Cumulative propellant produced and stored shall reach 1,400 t before crew departure.
At least two independent Sabatier chains shall exist on the surface before crew.
Installed generation shall reach 1.5 MWe or more before crew arrival.
A full crewed-return propellant load shall be produced and stored before the uncrewed return flight, which consumes a real load.

windows.objective: First crew: 12 land with pilots for everything the village scales

requirements_closed.statement: At least 5 habitat modules (rigid or inflatable) shall be on the surface before crew arrival.
Pre-landed consumable caches of 90 t or more shall be on the surface before crew.

windows.objective: Village: past the 110-person survival floor; scale the F3 pilots
Town: 500+; Gen-3 closure; electronics-fab pilot at earliest window
Settlement: 1,000+ adults (NSS milestone), three synods early
